task:
Ticket 0079 — atom 0 (runner core): model the mid-run founder-decision wait as a durable PARKED/held state instead of an ordinary directive poll governed by orchestrationMs.

BACKGROUND (read first, do not patch from a guess):
- packages/core/src/runner/founder-stop.ts already defines the durable park/resume contract: `held` run status, `resume-state.json`, `FounderHeldError`, `ResumeState` (pre-dispatch | during-exec | pre-verdict), `writeResumeState`/`readResumeState`.
- packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts already has `holdRun(park)` (writes resume-state, records `run-held`, sets status `held`, returns) and resumes a `held` run when `runRun` is called with `input.resumeRunId` (it asserts the run status is `held` — see line ~411 `Cannot resume run ... expected held`).
- THE BUG: when Oscar writes an `ask-founder-continue` directive, the runner sets `pendingFounderContinue` and loops back to `awaitDirectiveOrFounderHold(directivePath, n)`, which calls `io.awaitDirective(..., { timeoutMs: t.orchestrationMs, ... })`. After the 4h backstop it throws and the catch at the loop top calls `return await fail('directive-timeout', ...)`. A founder answer (directive-N.json) written after that fault lands in a dead run (run_272 shape).

WHAT TO CHANGE (runner core only this atom):
1. Establish ONE source-of-truth predicate for "this wait is awaiting founder resolution (park, do not directive-timeout)". Put it in a single owner module (founder-stop.ts is the natural home, or a small new module it re-exports) and export it; do NOT inline a second copy of this judgment anywhere. This atom only needs the predicate to cover the mid-run `ask-founder-continue` lane, but design the predicate/signature so the later lanes (post-wrap awaiting-founder, ticket-close confirmation, archive confirmation) can reuse the SAME owner — leave a clear extension seam, do not pre-wire those lanes here.
2. When the next-directive wait is a founder-decision wait (pendingFounderContinue active), the runner must PARK the run via the existing `held`/resume-state machinery (a pre-dispatch ResumeState at the next directive's atom number) rather than poll with `t.orchestrationMs`. The pending founder question/next-directive-path must be captured durably so a resume re-enters the same awaiting-founder atom and reads the founder's directive-N.json. A pending founder decision must NEVER end as `directive-timeout` because the founder did not answer within orchestrationMs.
3. Preserve the existing explicit `founder-stop.json` behavior and all current resume paths (pre-dispatch/during-exec/pre-verdict) exactly — do not regress them.
4. The late-answer guard must hold by construction: resuming requires status `held` (already enforced). Confirm (and test) that a late directive-N.json cannot be accepted into a `failed`/ended run as a live continuation — the run_272 shape.

OUT OF SCOPE for this atom (I will delegate next): daemon/Oz-CLI resume wiring that watches for the founder answer and relaunches with resumeRunId; dashboard/run-status/ticket-close-gate/archive-confirmation surface agreement; post-wrap awaiting-founder and archive/ticket-close park lanes. Do not touch those surfaces beyond the shared predicate seam. Do not weaken any existing test or guard into a tautology.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE (must all hold):
- A unit/integration test proves: with `ask-founder-continue` pending and the directive wait exceeding `orchestrationMs`, the run does NOT fault `directive-timeout` — it ends `held` with a durable resume-state marking the awaiting-founder atom.
- A test proves: a founder answer (directive-N.json) written after that interval, fed through the resume path (resumeRunId), resumes the parked run and is consumed as the live continuation.
- A test proves the run_272 shape cannot recur: a late directive-N.json written into a `failed` run is NOT accepted as a live continuation (resume rejects a non-held run).
- The single founder-resolution predicate has exactly one definition; no parallel copy.
- `pnpm -w typecheck` (or the repo's typecheck) passes, and the packages/core runner test suite passes (e.g. `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` or the equivalent the repo uses). Report the exact commands you ran and their real exit codes/output. MUST NOT break any existing runner, founder-stop, or status test.

Match surrounding code style and ADR conventions (this is ADR-0037 founder-stop / resume territory). Keep the change minimal-surface and elegant: reuse holdRun/resume-state, do not invent a parallel hold mechanism.

Ticket 0079 — atom 1 (predicate unification): make ONE source of truth identify 'a run awaiting founder resolution', covering the new mid-run parked `held` state plus the existing post-wrap lanes, and replace the duplicated predicates. Read the code before changing; do not patch from a guess.

WHY: Atom 0 made a mid-run `ask-founder-continue` wait park as run status `held` (durable/resumable). But the daemon's notion of 'awaiting founder' is the set `AWAITING_FOUNDER_STATUSES = {'awaiting-founder','awaiting-archive-confirmation'}` which is DUPLICATED in two files and does NOT include `held`. So a founder-decision-parked run is invisible to the ticket-close gate, nudge suppression, finalize, and status surfacing. The ticket requires: 'One source of truth identifies awaiting founder resolution across mid-run questions, post-wrap awaiting-founder, ticket-close confirmation, archive confirmation... Do not add parallel predicates for each caller.'

KNOWN OWNERS / SITES (re-grep the live tree to confirm and find any I missed — do not trust these line numbers blindly):
- packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:~646 `const AWAITING_FOUNDER_STATUSES = new Set([...])` (used by finalizeAwaitingFounderRun, finalizeAwaitingFounderRunsForTicket).
- packages/daemon/src/ticket-close-gate.ts:~3 a SECOND byte-identical `AWAITING_FOUNDER_STATUSES` copy.
- packages/core/src/runner/status.ts maps `held`→phase `awaiting-founder` already (~lines 118-134); confirm and keep consistent.
- packages/core/src/runner/founder-stop.ts:`isAwaitingFounderResolution` (atom 0) operates on a FounderResolutionWait marker, NOT a run status — leave it as the marker-level predicate; this atom adds the STATUS-level owner.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Create ONE exported owner predicate over run status in core (the natural home is packages/core/src/runner/status.ts, exported through the package index the daemon already imports) e.g. `isAwaitingFounderResolution(status: RunStatus): boolean` returning true for `'held'`, `'awaiting-founder'`, `'awaiting-archive-confirmation'`. This is the single 'is this run parked awaiting founder resolution?' predicate used for: ticket-close-gate blocking, nudge suppression, dashboard/Oz status surfacing, and 'do not directive-timeout' recognition.
2. Delete BOTH duplicated `AWAITING_FOUNDER_STATUSES` sets (launcher.ts and ticket-close-gate.ts) and have every consumer import/use the one owner. An automated check (a test) must fail if a second local copy of this status set reappears.
3. CRITICAL — preserve distinct finalize semantics, do NOT collapse them: `finalizeAwaitingFounderRun` sets a run to `completed`. A `held` run is RESUMABLE; finalizing it to `completed` on ticket close would silently discard resumable founder-decision work. So the FINALIZE-TO-COMPLETED action must remain narrower — it must apply ONLY to the post-wrap terminal lanes (`'awaiting-founder'`, `'awaiting-archive-confirmation'`), NOT to `'held'`. Represent that as its own clearly-named concept/constant (one owner per concept — recognition vs. finalize-action are two concepts), with a comment explaining why `held` is recognized-as-awaiting-founder but NOT finalize-to-completed. Add a test proving a `held` run is recognized by the awaiting-founder predicate yet is NOT clobbered to `completed` by finalize/ticket-close.

OUT OF SCOPE this atom (next atoms): the founder-answer→resume relaunch wiring; weaving the founder's answer into the resumed Oscar; regression tests for the post-wrap/archive lanes not directive-timing-out. Do not build those here. Do not touch oz-host/oz-chat answer handling.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE:
- Exactly one definition of the awaiting-founder-resolution status predicate; both old duplicated sets gone; a test fails if a duplicate reappears.
- The predicate returns true for held / awaiting-founder / awaiting-archive-confirmation.
- The ticket-close gate now blocks while a `held` founder-decision run exists (test it).
- finalize/ticket-close does NOT change a `held` run's status (test it) — only the post-wrap lanes finalize to completed (existing behavior preserved; test still green).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` (or repo equivalents) pass; the @cocoder/core and @cocoder/daemon test suites pass. Report the exact commands, real exit codes, and any newly added test names. MUST NOT break existing launcher/ticket-close/status tests.

Keep it minimal-surface and elegant; reuse existing imports/index exports rather than inventing a new module if status.ts is the right home.

Ticket 0079 — atom 2 (runner-side resume mechanics): make a mid-run founder-decision `held` run actually resumable end-to-end at the core/runner layer, channel-agnostic. Read the code first; do not patch from a guess.

WHY: Atom 0 parks an `ask-founder-continue` wait as a `held` run with resume-state {park:'pre-dispatch', founderResolution:{question, nextDirectivePath, ...}}. On resume (runRun called with input.resumeRunId), the runner correctly points its directive wait at `nextDirectivePath`. BUT two gaps make resume non-functional: (1) the resumed Oscar is spawned with buildOrchestratorPrompt firstDirectivePath = `directive-0.json` (runner.ts ~line 578) even though the runner is polling `nextDirectivePath` (e.g. directive-2.json) — so Oscar writes the wrong file; (2) the resumed Oscar receives NO context about the founder question or the founder's answer (assembleRunInput only loads pickup for the legacy resumeFromRunId path, not resumeHeldRunId), so it cannot author the continuation directive.

WHAT TO CHANGE (packages/core only this atom):
1. firstDirectivePath on resume: compute the resumed first-directive path from the resume-state directive index (the same index logic already added in atom 0: `resumedDirectiveIndex ?? n`) and pass THAT to buildOrchestratorPrompt instead of the hardcoded `directive-0.json`. Compute it near where resumeState is read so it is available when the Oscar launch prompt is built (the prompt is built well before the loop). For a fresh run it stays `directive-0.json`; for any resume it is `directive-<resumedIndex>.json`. This also fixes plain founder-stop pre-dispatch resumes, which is correct.
2. Carry the founder's answer into the resumed run: add an optional field to RunInput, e.g. `resumeFounderAnswer?: string | null`. When resuming a pre-dispatch park whose `founderResolution.kind === 'ask-founder-continue'`, weave a clear section into the resumed Oscar's launch prompt (extend buildOrchestratorPrompt or add a focused prompt builder) that states: the question Oscar previously asked (`founderResolution.question`), the founder's answer (`resumeFounderAnswer`), and an instruction to incorporate the answer and write the next directive to the resumed firstDirectivePath (delegate if concrete in-priority work remains, else wrapup) — mirroring the existing buildFounderContinueDispatch wording (prompts.ts ~621) but as resumed-launch context, not a live pane send. If `resumeFounderAnswer` is absent on such a resume, fall back gracefully (state the question and that the founder's answer should be incorporated) — do NOT crash.
3. Do NOT add a daemon answer channel, CLI command, route, or Oz-chat hook in this atom — that is atom 3. Keep this purely in packages/core (runner.ts, prompts.ts, RunInput type) so it is unit-testable via resumeRunId + resumeFounderAnswer.
4. Preserve all existing resume paths (during-exec, pre-verdict, pre-dispatch-with-directive, founder-stop) and the atom-0/atom-1 behavior unchanged.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE (core tests):
- A test resumes a founder-decision `held` run (built by the existing atom-0 park flow) with `resumeRunId` + `resumeFounderAnswer`, and proves: the resumed Oscar's launch prompt contains the prior question, the founder's answer, and the CORRECT next-directive path (the nextDirectivePath, not directive-0.json); and the run consumes the founder-answer directive written at that path and continues to completion. (Assert on the prompt text captured via the existing fake session host / driver, the same way other prompt-content tests do.)
- A test proves firstDirectivePath is `directive-0.json` for a fresh run and `directive-<n>.json` for a resume.
- A test proves a founderResolution resume with NO resumeFounderAnswer does not crash (graceful fallback prompt).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` passes and the full @cocoder/core test suite passes. Report exact commands, real exit codes, and new test names. MUST NOT break existing runner/prompts/status/founder-stop tests.

Keep it minimal-surface and elegant; reuse founderResolution from resume-state as the single source for question+nextDirectivePath, and reuse the existing buildFounderContinueDispatch phrasing rather than inventing a divergent second contract.

Ticket 0079 — atom 3 (daemon founder-answer channel + resume wiring): let the founder answer a parked mid-run decision through the existing Oz channel, which resumes the held run WITH the answer; reject a stale answer to recovery instead of a dead path. Read oz-host.ts, oz-chat.ts, launcher.ts, routes.ts first; mirror the existing `nudge` tool + `resumeRun` patterns. Do not patch from a guess.

BACKGROUND: Atoms 0-2 landed. A mid-run `ask-founder-continue` parks the run as status `held` with resume-state.json `founderResolution:{kind:'ask-founder-continue',question,askedAtDirectivePath,nextDirectivePath}`. Core `runRun` accepts `resumeRunId` + `resumeFounderAnswer` and weaves the question+answer into the resumed Oscar (RunInput.resumeFounderAnswer already exists in core). Daemon `resumeRun(ctx,runId)` (launcher.ts ~1226) calls `launchRun(...,{resumeHeldRunId})`. `assembleRunInput`/`launchRun` opts do NOT yet thread `resumeFounderAnswer`. `isAwaitingFounderResolutionStatus` is exported from @cocoder/core. The Oz tool list is in oz-host.ts (~line 226 toolInstructions, ToolName ~line 64); Oz command dispatch is oz-chat.ts `executeOzCommand` with the `OzChatOps` interface; the `nudge` tool is the closest existing analog to copy.

WHAT TO CHANGE (packages/daemon; thread one new opt through to core):
1. Thread the answer through the EXISTING single resume path — do NOT create a second resume lane. Add `resumeFounderAnswer?: string | null` to the `launchRun`/`assembleRunInput`/`buildRunInput` opts (launcher.ts ~208-329) and pass it into RunInput alongside `resumeRunId: opts.resumeHeldRunId` (RunInput.resumeFounderAnswer is already consumed by core). Extend `resumeRun` to accept an optional founder answer (e.g. `resumeRun(ctx, runId, opts?: {founderAnswer?: string|null})`) and forward it to launchRun. The plain POST /runs/:id/resume (no answer) must keep working unchanged for founder-stop held runs.
2. Add a founder-facing capture entry `requestFounderAnswer(ctx, runId, answer)` in launcher.ts that:
   - 404 if the run is missing.
   - Reads resume-state.json (readResumeState on the run dir). REJECT (409, clear recovery message) unless the run is `held` AND its resume-state has `founderResolution.kind === 'ask-founder-continue'`. This is the stale-answer guard: a late answer must NOT be written into a non-held / already-resolved / failed run — return a recovery error telling the founder to check `status`, and write NOTHING into the run dir. (Satisfies: 'a late founder answer cannot be written into a dead run's artifact path; it must resume the parked run or route to an explicit recovery surface.')
   - 400 if the answer text is empty/blank.
   - Persist the answer durably into the run dir as `founder-answer.json` (mirror the nudge atomicWriteJson pattern; resolveLocalRunDir ?? localRunDir) for audit/durability, record an event (e.g. `founder-answer-recorded`).
   - Then call `resumeRun(ctx, runId, {founderAnswer: answer})` and return its result.
3. Expose it through the founder channels, mirroring existing wiring:
   - Oz tool: add a `founder-answer` tool (ToolName + toolInstructions usage line + isToolName check + a validateFounderAnswerTool mirroring validateNudgeTool), add the command kind to oz-chat.ts (`OzCommand`/executable union + `OzChatOps.requestFounderAnswer` + a handler in executeOzCommand mirroring the nudge handler). Tool shape: `founder-answer {"runId":"...","answer":"..."}`.
   - HTTP route: add `POST /runs/:id/founder-answer` with body `{answer}` dispatching to `requestFounderAnswer` (mirror the ticket-close-confirmation route at routes.ts ~1045), so dashboard/CLI have parity.

OUT OF SCOPE this atom (atom 4): regression coverage that post-wrap awaiting-founder / ticket-close / archive waits don't directive-timeout, the run_272 end-to-end shape test, and the ADR/doc note + ticket close. Do not touch the runner core beyond threading the opt.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE:
- A daemon test: build a `held` run with an `ask-founder-continue` resume-state, call `requestFounderAnswer` (and/or the Oz `founder-answer` command and the HTTP route), and prove it persists founder-answer.json, calls launchRun with `{resumeHeldRunId, resumeFounderAnswer:<answer>}`, and returns 202.
- A daemon test: a founder answer to a run that is NOT held (e.g. failed/completed, or held without an ask-founder-continue marker) is REJECTED (409 recovery), writes NOTHING into the run dir, and does NOT relaunch — the run_272 'late answer into a dead run' shape.
- A daemon test: empty answer → 400.
- Oz-chat test: `founder-answer` parses and dispatches to `requestFounderAnswer` (mockOps pattern).
- One resume code path only (no duplicated launch logic); plain resume (no answer) still passes its existing test.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck`, and the @cocoder/daemon test suite (plus @cocoder/core if touched) pass. Report exact commands, real exit codes, new test names. MUST NOT break existing resume/nudge/oz-chat/mutations tests.

Keep it minimal-surface and elegant; reuse readResumeState, isAwaitingFounderResolutionStatus, atomicWriteJson, and the nudge/resume patterns rather than inventing new mechanisms.

Ticket 0079 — atom 4 (regression coverage for the remaining acceptance bullets): pin that the post-wrap / confirmation founder-resolution lanes do NOT behave as ordinary directive-timeout waits, and add one cohesive guard tying the run_272 narrative together. TESTS ONLY — do not change production behavior. Read the existing tests first so you ADD coverage rather than duplicate; do not weaken any test.

ALREADY COVERED (do NOT re-add — reference them, build on the same fixtures):
- packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts: `ask-founder-continue parks instead of timing out the next directive wait`; `resuming a parked founder decision consumes the founder answer directive...`; `late directive written into a failed run is rejected by resume status enforcement`; the firstDirectivePath/resume-prompt tests.
- packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts: `POST /runs/:id/founder-answer records the answer and resumes with it`; `...rejects stale, non-marker, and blank answers without relaunching`.

WHAT TO ADD (the ticket's still-unproven acceptance sub-bullets — 'post-wrap awaiting-founder, ticket close confirmation, and archive confirmation waits do not timeout as directive waits'):
1. Post-wrap `awaiting-founder`: a runner-level test proving that when Oscar writes a `wrapup` whose validated closeout needs a founder decision (Run Status awaiting-founder), the run reaches a TERMINAL `awaiting-founder` status with a `run-end` event and runRun RETURNS — it does NOT enter an orchestrationMs directive poll and does NOT emit `directive-timeout`, even when the orchestration timeout is tiny. Use the existing wrapup/closeout fixtures (deriveWrapupRunStatus / the awaiting-founder wrap path already exercised in runner.test.ts) and assert no `directive-timeout` event and the run is not awaiting a further directive. If the proper layer for the wrap-status derivation is the founder-closeout/status unit, pin it there instead — choose the layer that actually owns the behavior, and say which in the test name.
2. Ticket-close confirmation wait: a daemon-level test (packages/daemon/tests) proving a run parked awaiting ticket-close confirmation sits in its parked status awaiting `requestTicketCloseConfirmation` and is NOT governed by / does not fault on an orchestrationMs directive wait. Reuse the ticket-close-gate / confirmation fixtures already in the daemon tests.
3. Archive confirmation wait: same shape as #2 for `awaiting-archive-confirmation` / `requestArchiveConfirmation` — prove the parked confirmation wait is not a directive-timeout wait.
4. One cohesive named regression guard (name it for ticket 0079 / run_272, e.g. `ticket 0079: a founder decision wait survives past orchestrationMs and resumes, and cannot revive a failed run`) that asserts the end-to-end invariant in one place: a founder-decision park survives well past a tiny orchestrationMs as `held` (never `directive-timeout`), a later founder answer resumes it to completion, AND a late answer into a failed run is rejected. Compose it from the existing helpers; it is the ticket's single acceptance proof, so make the assertions explicit and readable.

SCOPE: packages/core/tests/** and packages/daemon/tests/** only. Do NOT edit production source, governance, or docs (the ADR/governance note is handled in Oscar's support lane separately). If writing a meaningful test reveals that one of these lanes ACTUALLY can directive-timeout (a real bug, not just missing coverage), STOP and report it in your completion notes with the evidence instead of papering over it — do not modify production code under a tests-only atom.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE:
- New tests exist for sub-bullets 1-4 above, each asserting the absence of `directive-timeout`/fault and the presence of the correct parked/terminal status, using real code paths (not trivially-passing mocks).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` both pass (report exact commands, real exit codes, and the new test names). MUST NOT weaken or delete existing tests.
- Typecheck both packages.

Keep the tests elegant and intention-revealing; reuse existing fixtures/helpers rather than rebuilding harnesses.
